entry_fee: 100-400 THB depending on orchard (all-you-can-eat buffet tours around 400 THB)
best_time_to_visit: May to June — peak durian season when Monthong and Chanee varieties are at their sweetest and most abundant

description.en:
## Overview

Chanthaburi province is Thailand's undisputed fruit capital, producing more durian than any other region in the country. The lush, rain-fed hills surrounding the city support vast orchards of durian, mangosteen, rambutan, langsat, and dozens of other tropical fruits. During the April-July harvest season, several family-run orchards open their gates to visitors for guided tours, tastings, and all-you-can-eat fruit buffets that draw foodies from across Thailand and beyond.

## History

Fruit cultivation in Chanthaburi dates back centuries, benefiting from the province's ideal combination of volcanic soil, heavy rainfall, and proximity to the coast. The durian industry exploded in the late 20th century as Thailand became the world's largest exporter of the fruit. Today, Chanthaburi hosts an annual Fruit Festival each May, celebrating the harvest with parades, contests for the best durian, and enormous open-air fruit markets.

## What to See

Most orchard tours begin with a shuttle ride through the plantation, where guides explain the cultivation process — from pollination by hand to the art of knowing exactly when a durian is ripe enough to fall. Visitors then sit down to taste premium varieties: Monthong (the most popular, with thick creamy flesh), Chanee (slightly more pungent), and Kradumthong (smaller but intensely sweet). Beyond durian, you will sample mangosteen — known as the "queen of fruits" — plus rambutan, longkong, and sala. Suan Issaree is one well-known organic farm offering a full durian and fruit buffet for around 400 THB per person including the tour.

## Practical Info

The fruit season runs from April to July, peaking in May-June. Most orchards require advance booking — ask your hotel or check local tourism offices for current options. Wear closed shoes suitable for walking on uneven orchard ground. Bring wet wipes and hand sanitizer — durian is messy. Prices range from 100 THB for a basic orchard visit to 400 THB for all-you-can-eat buffet experiences. Orchards are spread across the countryside, so private transport or a hired songthaew is recommended.
description.nl: De provincie Chanthaburi produceert meer durian dan waar ook in Thailand. Verschillende familieboomgaarden verwelkomen bezoekers voor rondleidingen tijdens het april-juli durianseizoen. Proef premium Monthong, Chanee en Kradumthong variëteiten direct van de boom, samen met mangosteen, rambutan, langsat en andere tropische vruchten in een van de meest vruchtbare fruitteeltgebieden ter wereld.

tips: Book a buffet-style orchard tour (around 400 THB) for the best experience — you will eat far more than the entry fee's worth
Visit in May during the annual Chanthaburi Fruit Festival for parades, contests, and the freshest durian of the season
Bring wet wipes and a change of clothes — durian juice stains and the smell clings to fabric

metaTitle.en: Chanthaburi Fruit Orchards - Durian Capital | Go2Thailand

metaDescription.en: Visit tropical fruit orchards in Chanthaburi. Taste premium durian straight from the tree in Thailand's fruit capital during the April-July season.
metaDescription.nl: Bezoek tropische fruitboomgaarden in Chanthaburi. Proef premium durian direct van de boom in Thailand's fruithoofdstad tijdens het april-juli seizoen.
